description_long: This is a small, single-engine, high-wing monoplane with a bright yellow fuselage and black registration markings 'SP-LCS' on both the wing and the side of the fuselage. The aircraft features a classic taildragger landing gear configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel at the rear. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, enclosed with clear windows, and appears to be designed for one or two occupants. The wings are straight and mounted high on the fuselage, providing good visibility below and to the sides. The tail section consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, both painted yellow. The propeller is mounted at the nose, indicating a piston engine configuration typical of light general aviation aircraft.
description: A small, yellow, high-wing monoplane with black markings, taildragger landing gear, and a front-mounted propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the high-mounted straight wings, nose-mounted propeller, and main landing gear.
description_view_1: Angled top-left view highlighting the yellow fuselage, cockpit windows, and black 'SP-LCS' markings on the wing and side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the taildragger gear, conventional tail assembly, and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Angled top-right view emphasizing the cockpit placement, propeller, and clear registration markings on the wing and fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or two-seat configuration.
